notes: This file is a PROPOSED schema. It is NOT the final lockfile. No mirror URLs or checksums are populated at this phase — Phase A only classifies provenance and licence.
Fields with `null` values are explicitly unresolved. Do not invent values.

Every entry references the ParseBench dataset (LlamaIndex; Apache-2.0). Individual entries may still be classified as reference-fetch-only when downstream copyright of the original public source cannot be verified.

The 'binary_url' field records the HuggingFace path pattern (docs/<category>/<basename>) based on the ParseBench README, not a mirror we control. Populating it during Phase B requires legal review + immutable mirror setup.

dataset_source.copyright_statement: All documents are sourced from public online channels. The dataset is released under the Apache 2.0 License. If there are any copyright concerns, please contact us via the GitHub repository. (ParseBench README, HuggingFace)
attribution_requirements: Retain the Apache-2.0 copyright notice + licence text (or a link to the licence) in any redistribution.
Cite Zhang et al., 2026 (arXiv:2604.08538) alongside any use of the dataset.
Note in the AksharaMD documentation that the assets originate from ParseBench, not from the AksharaMD project.
dataset_source.underlying_document_caveat: ParseBench asserts the *dataset compilation* is Apache-2.0, but the underlying documents were 'sourced from public online channels'. Individual documents may still have upstream copyright constraints that ParseBench's downstream Apache-2.0 declaration does not absolve. Each asset's `redistribution` field records this per-asset uncertainty.

assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); original document from public online channel, exact provenance not stated per-file
assets.attribution: Cite ParseBench (Zhang et al., 2026, arXiv:2604.08538) + retain Apache-2.0 notice.

page_level_ground_truth.notes: Phase-1 shipped metric: primary TP that drove the threshold=0.28 calibration. transition_rate ≈ 0.30 documented in validators/multicolumn.py:133.

assets.notes: Historical primary TP. Present at C:/Users/kalya/parsebench/data/docs/text/text_multicolumns__3colpres.pdf on the current workstation.

assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); underlying document appears to originate from IKEA marketing material — original copyright likely IKEA. Downstream Apache-2.0 declaration may not absolve upstream IKEA rights.
assets.attribution: Cite ParseBench + retain Apache-2.0 notice. If a specific IKEA copyright complaint surfaces, remove from mirror and rely on reference-fetch only.

page_level_ground_truth.notes: Known SPAN-LEVEL FN class per aksharamd/plugins/validators/multicolumn.py:163 — 'span-level interleaving undetectable at block level'. Not directly useful for block-level detector calibration; useful for measuring the gap that a span-level detector (C6) would need to close.

assets.notes: Local file present. Corporate-branded content raises stronger upstream-rights concern than public-domain government docs; keep as reference-fetch-only until Phase B legal review.

assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); underlying appears to be a page from *El País* (Spanish newspaper). Newspaper articles carry publisher copyright regardless of downstream Apache-2.0 assertion.
assets.attribution: Cite ParseBench + retain Apache-2.0 notice. Do NOT re-mirror the underlying content in an AksharaMD-branded location without El País permission.

page_level_ground_truth.notes: Known SPAN-LEVEL FN class per validators/multicolumn.py:163.

assets.notes: Newspaper content — publisher rights strongly recommend reference-fetch-only. Recorded in Phase 5 lock as span-level FN.

assets.notes: Critical for boundary verification. If eastbaytimes moves above the threshold under any candidate rule, the change is unshippable.
